question.text: What is the key difference between American-style and European-style options?

answer.text: American options can be exercised anytime before expiry, while European options can only be exercised at expiry
American options are traded in the US, while European options are traded in Europe
American options have higher premiums because they trade in dollars
American options are settled in cash, while European options are physically settled

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** American-style options can be exercised at ANY TIME up to and including the expiry date. European-style options can ONLY be exercised on the expiry date itself.
**The Trap:** The names "American" and "European" have NOTHING to do with geography. They refer to exercise style only.
**Cross-Exam Context:** In India, ALL exchange-traded options are EUROPEAN style. This is a frequently tested fact.
**Key Takeaway:** American = exercise anytime. European = exercise only at expiry. India uses European style.
